tournament.name: Regional Championship - South America - Devir Brasil

decks.notes: The deck focuses on using a combination of cards to quickly bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard, creating strong synergies and overwhelming the opponent with a high-powered board state. By utilizing cards like Deceit and Superior Spider-Man, the deck aims to reanimate key creatures like Doomsday Excruciator and Emeritus of Ideation. Supporting cards like Requiting Hex and Bitter Triumph help control the board while cards like Insatiable Avarice and Winternight Stories provide card advantage and additional resources for the combo. The deck is designed to efficiently assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming your opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing card draw and efficient removal, you aim to maintain tempo and pressure on your opponent. The key strategy is to play low-cost creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off to chip away at your opponent's life total, while utilizing spells like Burst Lightning and Prismari Charm to control the board. The inclusion of bounce spells like Boomerang Basics and extra turns with spells like Flow State allows you to maintain your aggression and disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the board through a variety of spells and enchantments, utilizing card draw and removal to outvalue opponents. The main win conditions are Jeskai Revelation and Broadside Barrage, allowing the player to draw cards and deal damage while maintaining control of the game. The deck also includes a mix of counterspells and board wipes to deal with threats. The mana base is designed to support the three colors of mana required for the deck, ensuring consistency in casting the necessary spells.

The deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo, supported by powerful buffs like Mightform Harmonizer and Earthbender Ascension. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures and removal spells like Erode. The key strategy is to control the board early on and maintain pressure throughout the game, using cards like Lumbering Worldwagon and Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam to provide additional threats. The mana base consists of a mix of lands to ensure consistent access to different colors for spells.

The main concept of this Izzet Control deck is to dominate the battlefield through a combination of powerful spells like Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson, while also using abilities like Abandon Attachments and Consult the Star Charts to manipulate the game in your favor. The deck is designed to control the flow of the game, anticipate your opponent's moves with cards like Three Steps Ahead, and ultimately utilize your resources to outplay and outlast your opponent. The focus is on strategic decision-making and resource management to secure victory.

The core idea of this deck revolves around utilizing control and disruption spells to manipulate the game state and outvalue the opponent. By playing a mix of counter spells, removal, and card draw, the player aims to control the board while setting up for powerful late-game threats like Hearth Elemental and Sunderflock. The deck also includes some unexpected cards like Traumatic Critique and Impractical Joke to add an element of surprise and disruption to throw off the opponent's plans. Overall, the strategy is to maintain control of the game while slowly building towards a win condition.

This deck focuses on quickly building a wide board presence with efficient creatures and then using Nature's Rhythm to enhance their abilities. By utilizing cards like Gene Pollinator and Spider Manifestation, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures. Brightglass Gearhulk provides protection for key creatures, while Ouroboroid and Craterhoof Behemoth offer strong finishers. The deck also includes some control elements with cards like Seam Rip and Meltstrider's Resolve to disrupt opponent strategies. Overall, this deck aims to be aggressive and proactive in its approach to winning the game.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo, while using cards like Mightform Harmonizer to pump them up. The deck also includes a few big threats like Mossborn Hydra and Surrak, Elusive Hunter to close out the game. Erode and Earthbender Ascension provide removal and disruption to control the battlefield. The mana base is designed for consistency, with Fabled Passage and Hushwood Verge ensuring the right colors are available. Escape Tunnel can help protect key creatures from removal spells.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card advantage. It aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources. Using efficient removal like Lightning Helix and Inevitable Defeat, along with card draw spells like Jeskai Revelation and Tablet of Discovery, the deck looks to outvalue the opponent in the long run. It also includes a mix of board wipes, counterspells, and versatile spells to adapt to different situations and maintain control throughout the match.

The main concept of this deck focuses on utilizing a mix of flying creatures and combat tricks to quickly overwhelm the opponent and deal damage efficiently. By combining evasive creatures like Momo, Friendly Flier and Sage of the Skies with cards like Quantum Riddler and Practiced Offense, the deck aims to maintain pressure and control the tempo of the game. Additionally, utility cards like Starfield Shepherd and Spider-Sense provide protection and resilience, while cards like Cosmogrand Zenith and Haliya, Guided by Light offer powerful finishers to close out games. The mana base includes versatile lands like Floodfarm Verge and Multiversal Passage to ensure smooth mana fixing.

This Golgari Control deck focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies and gaining card advantage with cards like Duress, Requiting Hex, and Strategic Betrayal. It also includes powerful removal spells like Maelstrom Pulse and Cruelclaw's Heist. The deck incorporates a mix of creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Firdoch Core for board presence and card draw, while also utilizing Professor Dellian Fel for additional card advantage. The mana base includes cards that can generate additional resources like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber and Wastewood Verge, allowing for flexibility and resilience in long games.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board through a combination of removal spells and counterspells, while simultaneously drawing cards and generating advantage. It aims to outvalue the opponent with efficient spells and card advantage, using powerful finishers like Gran-Gran and Firebending Lesson to close out the game. The deck also utilizes mana fixing and ramp to ensure that it can cast its spells consistently and efficiently. By controlling the game tempo and resource advantage, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast its opponents in order to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and aggressive playstyle using creatures like Bloodghast, Marauding Mako, and Iron-Shield Elf. Using cards like Requiting Hex and Erode to control the board and keep up the pressure. The deck also includes cards like Cool but Rude and Practiced Offense to maintain tempo and continuously apply pressure. The mana base is designed to provide consistency and enable aggressive plays. Overall, the strategy revolves around fast, efficient creatures and spells to quickly defeat the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing the Selesnya color combination to maximize efficient creatures and strong synergies. By playing powerful creatures like Badgermole Cub and Mossborn Hydra, ramping up with Llanowar Elves and Icetill Explorer, and disrupting opponents with cards like Erode and Earthbender Ascension, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and apply pressure. The inclusion of versatile cards like Sazh's Chocobo and Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam adds flexibility to adapt to different situations.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish board presence and overwhelm your opponent with efficient creatures and combat tricks. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo to put pressure on the opponent early on, paired with cards like Mightform Harmonizer and Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam to keep the pressure on, this Aggro deck aims to close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of Earthbender Ascension and Erode provides additional removal options to clear the way for your creatures to attack.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bring them back to the battlefield using reanimation spells. By using a combination of cards that support this strategy, such as Deceit, Superior Spider-Man, and Doomsday Excruciator, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures early in the game. Additionally, cards like Bitter Triumph and Stock Up provide card advantage and protection, while Winternight Stories and Emeritus of Ideation help with consistency and card selection. The deck also includes some disruption elements like Duress and Strategic Betrayal to control the opponent's plays, and uses lands like Gloomlake Verge and Watery Grave to support its color requirements.

The main concept of this Azorius Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of flying creatures and combat tricks. By utilizing powerful creatures like Momo, Friendly Flier and Sage of the Skies, along with support from cards like Starfield Shepherd and Springleaf Drum, this deck aims to establish a fast and aggressive board presence. Quantum Riddler and Aang, Swift Savior provide additional value and disruption, while cards like Erode and Seam Rip help control the opponent's board. The deck's mana base is built around ensuring consistent access to both white and blue mana sources.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent using efficient and aggressive creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off. The deck also includes a variety of spells like Burst Lightning and Spell Pierce to control the board and protect your creatures. Boomerang Basics and Impractical Joke provide additional disruption while Flow State and Stormchaser's Talent help to maintain card advantage and tempo. The mana base is optimized for consistency with a mix of lands and Multiversal Passage for fixing.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on controlling the board with cheap removal spells and disruptive creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off. The deck aims to quickly deploy threats and attack the opponent's life total before they can stabilize. Utilizing a mix of burn spells like Burst Lightning and tempo plays like Boomerang Basics, the deck looks to close out games quickly with high tempo plays and efficient creatures. The mana base provides flexibility and consistency, allowing the deck to smoothly curve out and apply pressure early on.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using a combination of cheap spells and creatures. The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Burst Lightning, Opt, and Stormchaser's Talent to control the board and keep up the pressure. Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off provide early aggression, while Colorstorm Stallion and Flow State help maintain tempo. The deck also includes some utility cards like Boomerang Basics and Get Out for additional control. Overall, the key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient aggressive gameplay.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost aggressive creatures and using efficient spells to control the board and burn the opponent down. It aims to maintain tempo and pressure by constantly deploying threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck utilizes a mix of card draw, counter spells, and direct damage to maintain advantage and close out games quickly. With a mixture of evasion, burn spells, and bounce effects, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and quick tempo. The deck focuses on using low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage quickly and efficiently. By utilizing cards like Burst Lightning, Slickshot Show-Off, and Eddymurk Crab, the deck aims to maintain board control and apply pressure on the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of counterspells like Spell Pierce and Prismari Charm allows for protection against opposing threats. Overall, the key strategy of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain a fast-paced offense while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on fast, aggressive play with a mix of creatures and spells to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The key strategy revolves around using cheap spells and creatures to chip away at the opponent's life total while maintaining control of the game with bounce effects and countermagic. By maintaining pressure on the opponent and disrupting their plans, the deck aims to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize and mount a comeback.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost spells and creatures, utilizing a combination of direct damage, card draw, and tempo manipulation. By efficiently casting cheap spells like Burst Lightning and Opt, the deck can maintain pressure while also controlling the board with bounce spells like Boomerang Basics. The inclusion of Stormchaser's Talent and Eddymurk Crab allows for aggressive strategies that can quickly close out games. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive playstyle with a balance of Islands, dual lands, and a Multiversal Passage for color fixing.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells and removal while utilizing the Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental to stabilize the board. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up for a big finish with cards like Traumatic Critique and Burst Lightning. The inclusion of Prismari Charm and Sleight of Hand helps to dig for key pieces while maintaining card advantage. Additionally, Winternight Stories and Impractical Joke provide some additional disruption and win condition options. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through careful planning and resource management.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of control spells and card draw to outvalue and outlast the opponent. By efficiently removing threats and drawing cards, the pilot aims to control the game and set up for powerful late-game plays like Jeskai Revelation or Into the Flood Maw. The inclusion of versatile removal spells like Pyroclasm and Sear allows for flexibility in handling various threats, while tablets like Tablet of Discovery provide extra card advantage. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistent access to all colors needed for the deck's spells and effects.

The deck focuses on assembling the Omniscience Combo using cards like Ashling, Rekindled and Kona, Rescue Beastie to generate mana and draw cards, while also protecting the combo with counterspells like Spell Pierce and Spell Snare. Additional creatures like Marang River Regent and North Wind Avatar help control the board while also providing extra value. The deck also includes support cards like Stock Up and Winternight Stories to maintain hand advantage and control the game. The goal is to quickly ramp into Omniscience and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells without paying their mana cost.

This deck focuses on using a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also incorporates cards that can help to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base includes a variety of dual lands to ensure consistent access to the deck's color requirements. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and varied array of threats while keeping them on the back foot with disruption and removal spells.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of low-cost creatures and powerful enchantments. By using cards like Badgermole Cub, Llanowar Elves, and Sazh's Chocobo, as well as Earthbender Ascension and Mightform Harmonizer, the deck aims to build a strong board presence early on. Additionally, the inclusion of escape abilities like Escape Tunnel and Fabled Passage allows for strategic maneuvering and evasion. Overall, the deck emphasizes fast and aggressive gameplay to dominate the battlefield and secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures from the graveyard, using a combination of cards such as Deceit and Superior Spider-Man. This allows for explosive plays and combos that can overwhelm opponents. In addition, the deck includes cards like Duress and Deadly Cover-Up to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect your own game plan. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the reanimation strategy, with a mix of Swamps and dual lands to ensure smooth mana production.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bringing them back onto the battlefield for a big play. By combining cards that fill the graveyard with cards that can reanimate creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of powerful creatures early in the game. Additionally, the deck includes cards that disrupt the opponent's strategy and provide protection for your own creatures, allowing for a versatile and adaptive playstyle.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bringing them back into play for huge swings in power and advantage. By using cards like Requiting Hex and Superior Spider-Man to fill the graveyard, followed by cards like Deceit and Emeritus of Ideation to bring them back, the deck can overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures early in the game. This strategy is supported by cards like Duress and Stock Up to control the opponent's hand and resources, granting the deck the opportunity to establish a dominant board presence.
